title: China moves to raise penalties on auditors as anti-fraud campaign deepens

China is moving to sharply increase penalties for auditors who sign off on fraudulent corporate accounts, widening a campaign against financial fraud by listed companies.
A draft amendment to the Certified Public Accountants Law – set for a second reading by the National People’s Congress Standing Committee, China’s top legislative body – would raise the maximum fine for issuing false audit reports to 10 times the gains accrued through fraud, double the present limit.
